rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF VINCENT GAGLIARDO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #8, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing, although the balance on hand in the Bank of America checking account as of 2/27/23 was not truly reconciled (neither the amount on hand nor the funds available for transfer to the administrator was not the amount shown on the bank statement), the costs the conservator reimbursed himself, as set forth in his 7th accounting, totaled $558.82, not $559.25, and some of the attorney costs should have been requested in the decedent's estate. Conservator's costs were shown on the liabilities schedule as $2,383.34, as also reported in the petition, which will be the amount allowed, not the unsupported amount in paragraph 4 of the prayer. The Court waives incomplete notice (no attachment to the Notice of Hearing regarding remote appearance procedures, as required by LRSF 14.1 D.6.), as the remote appearance procedures were sent to the same parties in the related case. Counsel should make a note of this, however, for future matters/hearings. NB: Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] When papers are not filed electronically, each paper presented for filing must contain two prepunched normal-sized holes, centered 21/2 inches apart and 5/8 inch from the top of the paper. [CRC 2.115.] This includes proposed orders. The Court will not ordinarily order what is not requested in a verified pleading. No SASE was provided; copies of signed orders cannot be returned to Counsel.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ROBERT PICASO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining Heirship
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Counsel to address the following procedural issues: 1. Under which authority the court can grant Anne Whiteman's shares to be distributed to her siblings when she was survived by a spouse. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JULIE HALL PINCHIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/27/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Please click on the link on the Probate Division's website that says "Click Here for Remote Appearance Proceedings for Department 202 and Department 204": https://www.sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate That is the information that must be attached to the Notice of Hearing. (2) Who is Denise Terrell? (3) It appears that the Santa Barbara County case has been closed. Are Alyssa Flores's Letters still valid? A copy of the order for final distribution may need to be filed to confirm who the heirs of Nancy Eileen (Patterson) Jordan were. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by 6/15/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (A supplement should stand on its own and not refer to issues posted in a tentative ruling. In particular, defective notice should simply be corrected and a proof of service of the corrected notice filed, not narrated in a supplement, and courtesy copies should just be delivered without stating in a pleading that they will be delivered.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE DIANE FRANCES WALSH REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/27/23. (No earlier availability.) An amendment requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] Did the former paralegal meet ALL of the requirements of Business & Professions Code Section 6450 while she was employed by the attorneys and billing in this matter, including complying with the continuing education requirements? NB: The 6/15/21 order appointing the successor trustees ordered that, while the trust remains under Court supervision, the successor co-trustees "are subject to the same terms and conditions as a conservator of the estate, notwithstanding any provisions of the Trust to the contrary." Fees should not have been paid without a prior Court order and the attorneys should not have received such payments. [CRC 7.755(a).] Deadline is 6/15/23 for providing a courtesy copy of (1) the proof of service of notice of the 6/27/23 hearing of the accounting petition and amendment thereto and (2) the attorney declaration regarding her former paralegal's qualifications to bill as a paralegal, and any other papers. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (There is no need to narrate in a pleading the history of prior hearings and reasons for continuances; that is part of the record, and it is not necessary to spend time drafting such information for inclusion in a supplement or amendment. Furthermore, a supplement or amendment should stand on its own and not be a "response" to issues raised by the Court.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GERARD O'LOUGHLIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/27/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) An amendment was filed on 3/20/23. An amendment requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] (2) Notice must include the language regarding remote appearance procedures. [LRSF 14.1 D.6.] (3) Under what authority was the 2015 Ford Transit Van transferred to Erick Deras Vasquez (for a loss of $2,000 to the estate)? No receipt has been filed. (4) The request to reimburse the executor is missing from the petition caption and prayer. [CRC 7.102.] (5) Exhibit A to the petition does not show full descriptions or payees for each transaction. (6) There was personal property tax payable by the estate? (7) A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed, including the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary, not just the account number. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.35 I.6.] (8) Funds in excess of the FDIC insured amount has been retained at one bank. No courtesy copies have been received by the examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] The petition was reviewed online as a ONE-TIME courtesy in hopes of not having to continue the hearing. Courtesy copies of the petition, any supplement or amendment thereto, and proofs of service MUST BE delivered to the assigned examiner by no later than 6/15/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL TODD DEBEER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order To Produce Decedent'S Google Account Contents
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. The petition requests the Court's order directing Google, as custodian of the decedent's digital assets, to disclose the contents stored in the decedent's Google accounts, presumably including the content of electronic communications sent or received by the decedent (as defined in Probate Code section 871(d)), in addition to a catalogue thereof (see sections 871(c) and 877) , even though there is no record evidencing the decedent's consent to disclosure of the content of electronic communications, one of the items required by section 876(d) to compel the custodian to disclose the content to the personal representative. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JILL FRANCES GRIFFIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/27/23. (No earlier availability.) 1) If the decedent's father died on 9/22/22, he also post-deceased the decedent. (No copy of the petitioner's "declaration" filed on 2/22/22 has been received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.]) The petitioner's personal search for heirs on Ancestry.com does not appear to be adequate. 2) The attorney's "declaration" filed on 4/6/22 also provides no supporting documentation for the allegations he, not his client, make. [CRC 7.103(c).] Regardless of anyone reportedly disinheriting his/her child, that child is an heir of this decedent if his sister is an heir of this decedent. 3) The petition caption shows that this is a Petition for Probate of Lost Will. This has never been amended, although the original will was located and lodged. 4) Item 3h(2) is checked, indicating the proposed personal representative is a nonresident of California, but he resides in San Francisco. This has not been corrected. 5) Item 6c of the petition is checked, indicating that Decedent was survived by a grandparent or grandparents, but no grandparent is listed in item 8 of the petition. 6) The copy of the "written instructions" attached to the petitioner's "declaration" filed on 4/6/22 is partly illegible and is not accompanied by a typed version. [LRSF 14.18 A.] Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (either a supplement or an amendment to the petition [CRC 7.3.]) must be received by the assigned examiner before 6/15/23. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The decedent's name was Jill Frances Griffin, not Jill Francis Griffin. Her mother was alleged to be Frances Powell Griffin, not Francis Powell Griffin. The decedent's maternal grandmother was alleged to be Amanda Thirston in the petitioner's "declaration" filed on 2/22/23. In the petitioner's "declaration" filed on 4/6/23, the maternal grandmother is identified as Amanda Thurston.)
